exercises.name: Battle Rope Inward Circle
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll start at the ground, scoop out around making a circle with your arms and coming in towards the middle.

2) While you’re doing so, keep yourself in a half squat to ensure a strong foundation.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Jumping Jack
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll be doing a jumping jack while holding on to the ropes.

2) When you perform the jumping jack, ensure that the arms come out laterally beside you rather than in front of you.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Outward Circle
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll make large circles starting from the middle going outward, scooping up and heading out again.

2) To set your base, get into a half squat and hold yourself there.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Overhead Press to Lateral Drop
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, it’ll be a combination between a shoulder press and a shoulder fly.

2. Hold the rope with both hands in front of your chest. This will be your starting position.

3. Raise both hands all the way up, then do a shoulder fly by moving your arms sideward, then back up again.

4. Back to starting position.

5. Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions

exercises.name: Battle Rope Plank Alternating Slams
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll be on top of the rope with your hand hold grip.

2) Maintain a quiet body that means abdominal engaged and hips not rocking back and forth or lifting up too high.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Power Slam
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, your goal is to send a single wave down the rope.

2) You’ll do so by lifting the rope up high and using your whole body momentum and body weight to slam the rope to the ground.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Russian Twist
exercises.alternateName: battle rope v sit core rotations
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your abdominal are engaged. You do so by shortening the distance between the bottom of your ribs and the top of your hips.

2) As you move the ropes left to right, you’ll want to move from your rib cage to help turn your body.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Side Slam
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, keeping your hands next to each other, you’ll alternate slamming the ropes to the right and to the left of your body.

2) Bend your torso and allow your legs to help out.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Side Slam Alternating Lunge
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll slam the rope alternating from the left to the right side of your body keeping your hands as close together as possible.

2) At the same time, in the best lunge position as possible, alternate and jump back and forth with the rope.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Sidewinder
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to keep your hands as close together as possible.

2) To perform the movement itself, you’ll want to sway back and forth in your hips as well as rotating in through your torso.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Single Arm Elbow Plank Waves
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain a quiet body that means abdominals engaged and hips not rocking back and forth.
 
2) Use one arm to send vertical waves down the rope.

3) When you’ve completed one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Single Arm Inward Circle
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll scoop along the floor.

2) Make a circle upward towards the air, then bring the arm down and inward.

3) Set a stable base by bending knees and going in to a half squat keeping an isolated or quiet body.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Single Arm Outward Circle
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll start at the bottom of the circle. Go up, in across your body an then slam it down out as you complete the circle.

2) Isolate your lower body by going to a half squat and holding yourself there so that all the work is done by the arm on the upper body.

3) When you’re done one side, switch to the other side and do the same number of reps or for the same duration of time.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Single Arm Wave
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, send repetitive waves down the rope just using one arm.

2) You can isolate it to make an upper body exercise OR include repeating squats to make it a full body exercise. 

3) Once you’re done one side, repeat the number of reps with the other arm.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Single Arm Wave Full Plank
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain a quiet body that means abdominal engaged and hips not rocking back and forth.

2) When you’ve completed one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Single Leg Snake
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll be on one foot. You’ll want to bend into a half squat position.

2) From there, you’ll put outward momentum on the rope as you slide the rope in and out along the floor like snakes.

3) When you’re done one foot, switch. Then do the other for the same amount of reps OR for the same duration of time.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Slam Squat Jump
exercises.description: 1) When performing this exercise, you’ll time the power slam of the ropes both happening at the same time, left and right hands with a jump from a squat.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Snake
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to set your base by getting into half squat position.

2) From there, you put momentum onto the rope outward as you relax and return your arms to center.

3) Push put like a Delt Fly repeatedly.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Star Jump
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll essentially do a jumping jack but before you touch down on the ground, your arms and legs will return to center.

2) You’ll want to keep the rope moving up laterally beside you rather than in front of your body.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Thruster
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, in the overhead position, you want to ensure your biceps are in line with your ears.

2) To make it a little more challenging, you can step back to have a little more resistance from the rope.

3) Keep in mind that you don’t want to lean back from your back so keep your abdominal tight when you’re at the top of the thruster.

exercises.name: Battle Rope Wave Jump Squat
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll move the rope alternating left to right in opposing directions.

2) At the same time, you’ll perform a jump squat.

exercises.name: BB Barbell Curtsy Lunge
exercises.description: BB Barbell Curtsy Lunge

exercises.name: Bear Crawl
exercises.description:
(1) Start in a position similar to a plank, but with one side of your body's arm and leg far apart from one another (both extended), and one side of your body's arm and leg close together (touching knee to elbow on that side if possible). 

(2) From here, keep your body high while staying as square to the ground with your hips and shoulders as possible.

(3) Next, simultaneously reach as far forward as you can with the hand that is furthest back (the one touching your knee), and step forward with the foot furthest back (the one stretched out). Keep your core engaged. 

(4) Repeat alternating sides until provided reps or distances are completed.

exercises.name: Bear Crawl Bird Dog
exercises.description:
1. Start on all fours, and lift your knees up off the ground about 1 inch. 

2. While holding this position, reach your opposing arm and leg to a fully extended position without losing your balance, or rocking side to side.

3. Slowly return to the start position under control, and repeat for desire number of repetitions. 

4. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Bear Plank
exercises.description:
1. Start on your hands and knees on the mat.

2. Point your toes into the floor, and lift your knees 1 inch off the floor. 

3. Keep your shoulders directly over top of your hands, hips over knees, and abdominals tight. 

4. Hold this position.

exercises.name: Bear Plank with Shoulder Tap
exercises.description:
Start on all fours, then lift your knees slightly off the ground so your hips are low and your back is flat, forming a bear plank position. Keep your core engaged and hips stable. 

Lift one hand off the ground and tap the opposite shoulder, then return it to the floor. Alternate sides, tapping each shoulder while minimizing any rotation in the hips or torso.

exercises.name: Bear Position Kettlebell Pull Through
exercises.description:
Start in a bear crawl position (hands and feet on the ground, knees bent). 

Reach under your body with one hand to pull the kettlebell across to the opposite side. 

This works the core, shoulders, and coordination.

exercises.name: Bear Squat to Downward Dog
exercises.description: From a bear squat, straighten your legs and lift your hips into a downward dog position.

exercises.name: Bear Squat to Push-Up
exercises.description:
Begin in a bear position with hands under shoulders, knees bent at 90 degrees, and hovering slightly above the ground. 

From this position, perform a squat by bringing your hips back slightly while keeping your knees off the ground. 

Return to the bear position and immediately perform a push-up, lowering your chest toward the floor and pressing back up.

exercises.name: Bear Squat to Spiderman
exercises.description: From a bear squat, step one foot forward into a deep lunge, then return and switch sides.

exercises.name: Bear Squat to Spinal Wave
exercises.description: Perform a bear squat, then transition into a spinal wave by rounding and extending your spine.

exercises.name: Bear Stance Single Arm Row
exercises.description:
Start in a bear crawl position with your knees slightly off the ground and your arms straight. 

Hold a dumbbell in each hand, and row them towards your torso, keeping your back flat and core tight. 

Lower and repeat.

exercises.name: Bear to Stand
exercises.description: From a bear crawl position, press through your feet and extend into a standing position.

exercises.name: Bear to Step Through
exercises.description: From a bear position, step one leg forward and rotate your torso, keeping the movement fluid.

exercises.name: Bear to T-Rotation
exercises.description: From a bear position, rotate your torso and reach one arm toward the ceiling. Alternate sides.

exercises.name: Bear to Underswitch
exercises.description: Start in a bear crawl stance and rotate one leg under your body, transitioning to a crab position.

exercises.name: Bear Underswitch to Crab Reach
exercises.description: Perform a bear underswitch, then reach one arm overhead to extend into a crab reach.

exercises.name: Bear Walk Out to Push-Up
exercises.description: Start on all fours, then walk your hands forward while keeping your knees slightly off the ground until your body is in a push-up position. Perform a push-up, then walk your hands back toward your feet and return to the starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Beat Swings
exercises.description:
1. Start by gripping a pull up bar with a grip slightly wider than shoulder-width. 

2. Initiate the movement with the shoulders, pushing your body into an arched back position. 

3. From the arched position, lift your hips and legs forwards, driving the body back behind the bar.

4. Continue alternating between the arched back position and the "hollow" position, aiming to time the movement to keep momentum.
